metadata.source: Meridian artifacts (MRL, Control List, CSC-01, CSP SOC 2 report)

controls.title: User Entity Workspace - IP Whitelisting
controls.descriptionText: As part of user entity onboarding, the Engineering team restricts access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configurations within Zephyr Platform backend infrastructure to determine whether the Engineering team had restricted access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).

Inspected the configurations within Quanta Portfolio backend infrastructure to determine whether the Engineering team had restricted access to the production workspace of each user entity by whitelisting their IP(s).
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of clients using Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products.
For a sample of clients, screenshot showing whitelisting of client IPs.

controls.title: Access Review - AWS
controls.descriptionText: The CloudOps team performs a review of the administrative access to AWS, Okta, MongoDB, Datadog, PagerDuty, DevRev, and GitHub on a quarterly basis. Users along with their privileges are reviewed with the help of CISO within a Jira ticket. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The CISO reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of quarters, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team performed a review of the administrative access to AWS, Okta, MongoDB, Datadog, PagerDuty, DevRev, and GitHub and whether the CISO provided sign off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarters, complete access review tickets for AWS for all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.

controls.title: Single sign on - Cloud and supporting tools
controls.descriptionText: Access to AWS, GitHub, PagerDuty, DevRev and Datadog is restricted using single sign on (SSO) enforced by Okta.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configuration within Okta to determine whether access to AWS, GitHub, PagerDuty, DevRev and Datadog is restricted through Okta SSO.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to AWS is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to Github is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to PagerDuty is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to DevRev is restricted through Okta
Screenshot from Okta demonstrating that access to Datadog is restricted through Okta

controls.title: Privilege Access Management - AWS
controls.descriptionText: Privileged access to AWS resources hosting Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio infrastructure is restricted and governed through AWS IAM Policies.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations to determine whether privileged access to AWS resources hosting Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio infrastructure was restricted and governed through AWS IAM Policies.
evidenceRequirementTexts: AWS IAM configuration screenshots for all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.

controls.title: Backup Configuration - AWS - Zephyr Platform
controls.descriptionText: AWS Backup managed service is used by Meridian for backup of AWS services. Full backup of AWS RDS is scheduled every day and is retained for 7 days. Alerts, as applicable, are defined in case of failure of backup.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether full backup of AWS RDS was scheduled everyday and was retained for 7 days.

Additionally, inspected the configuration of AWS Backup to determine whether alerts were configured to be logged into AWS CloudWatch.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from AWS showing the backup schedule for Zephyr Platform.
Screenshot from AWS showing the retention period of backup for Zephyr Platform.
AWS Backup configuration screenshots showing that backup‑related alerts are logged and monitored via AWS CloudWatch.

controls.title: Backup Configuration - AWS - Quanta Portfolio
controls.descriptionText: AWS Backup managed service is used by Meridian for backup of AWS services. Full backup of EBS volume for block storage, SQL and NoSQL database instances is scheduled every day and is retained for 93 days. Alerts, as applicable, are defined in case of failure of backup.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether full backup of EBS volume was scheduled everyday and was retained for 93 days.

Additionally, inspected the configuration of AWS Backup to determine whether alerts were configured to be logged into AWS CloudWatch.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from AWS showing that backup of EBS volume for block storage, SQL and NoSQL database instances is scheduled everyday for all Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.
Screenshot from AWS showing the retention period of backup of RBS volume for Quanta Portfolio products.
AWS Backup configuration screenshots showing that backup‑related alerts are logged and monitored via AWS CloudWatch

controls.title: Backup Replication - AWS
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured the backup copies of AWS RDS and EBS Volume to be automatically replicated across other regions.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether replication to other regions of backup copies of AWS RDS and EBS Volume was enabled.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from AWS showing replication to other regions of backup copies of AWS RDS was enabled.
Screenshot from AWS showing replication to other regions of backup copies of AWS EBS Volume was enabled.

controls.title: Availability Zones (AZ) - Cloud
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured the AWS of Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio to utilize multiple geographically separate availability zones.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether AWS resources were utilized across multiple availability zones.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from AWS showing AWS resources were utilized across multiple availability zones for all Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.

controls.title: Backup Restoration Tests
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs backup restoration and recovery tests of Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio databases on a quarterly basis. Results of test are recorded within a Jira ticket. Failures in restoration, if any, are fixed by taking appropriate corrective actions and recording / documenting the same within the Jira ticket. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within the Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of quarters, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps Team performed backup restoration and recovery tests of backed up Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio databases and whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of quarters, JIRA ticket showing that backup restoration tests were performed.
For a sample of quarters, JIRA ticket showing that Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.

controls.title: Disaster Recovery (DR) Drill
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, Meridian conducts disaster recovery simulation / tabletop exercise for the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products and its supporting infrastructure to test its business continuity and disaster recovery measures and determine the effectiveness of mitigating controls for business disruption risks. Post the activity, DR drill report is prepared by the CloudOps team, highlighting the tests conducted, test results, and improvement areas. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provides approval within a Jira ticket once the activity has concluded.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the DR drill report for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether a disaster recovery simulation / tabletop exercise was conducted and whether the drill documented controls and test results for infrastructure availability, system and data availability, uninterrupted process flow and training of personnel to handle disaster.

Additionally, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual DR drill report for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products.
JIRA ticket showing the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign-off.

controls.title: Data Purging
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled configurations to automatically purge (permanently delete) data within AWS based on expiry of lifecycle rules.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether data within AWS was automatically purged based on expiry of lifecycle rules.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing Lifecycle rules within AWS showing the schedule as to when data is set to expire Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.
Screenshot showing that data was set to be permanently deleted (purged) upon expiry withing AWS for all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.

controls.title: Data Flow Diagram
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a data flow diagram for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio to identify and track the flow of data between internal components and external entities, including data sources, transmission paths, and system boundaries.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the data flow diagram to determine whether the flow of data between internal components and external entities, including data sources, transmission paths, and system boundaries were tracked.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Data flow diagram for Zephyr Platform.
Data flow diagram for Quanta Self-Serve.
Data flow diagram for Quanta Analyze.
Data flow diagram for Quanta Discover.
Data flow diagram for X-Cosnole.
Data flow diagram for Quanta Assist hosted on AWS.
Data flow diagram for Quanta Capture NG.

controls.title: Web Application Firewall
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled WAF between Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products and the internet to monitor, filter, and block HTTPS traffic as well as any potential attacks such as distributed denial of service (DDoS) on its web application.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether AWS WAF was enabled between Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products and the internet to monitor, filter, and block HTTPS traffic as well as any potential attacks on its web application.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing AWS WAF was enabled between Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances and the internet.

controls.title: Network Address Translation
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented network address translation (NAT) within its VPCs using AWS NAT Gateway to manage and mask internal IPs, and to securely route outbound traffic from internal systems to external networks.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether AWS NAT Gateway was configured to manage and mask internal IPs, and to securely route outbound traffic from internal systems to external networks.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of NAT Gateways configured within all Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances VPCs showing that NAT Gateways was configured on the Prod VPC.

controls.title: External Traffic Restrictions
controls.descriptionText: Access to the AWS cloud environment hosting Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products and supporting infrastructure is restricted to authorized personnel using AWS Security Groups.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether AWS Security Groups was configured to restrict access to AWS Console and the services within the AWS cloud environment by whitelisting authorized IPs.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot of AWS Security Groups configured within Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances VPCs showing multiple inbound and outbound rules.

controls.title: AWS Security Groups - ruleset review
controls.descriptionText: CloudOps team performs AWS Security Groups ruleset review on an annual basis by extracting the AWS Security Groups ruleset from the AWS platform and reviewing them with the help of Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team to identify stale / inaccurate / missing rules. In case of any discrepancies, the CloudOps team takes necessary corrective actions. The Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team reviews the result of the review within the Jira ticket and provides sign off.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the AWS Security ruleset review Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team performed a review of AWS Security Groups ruleset and whether the Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign off.
evidenceRequirementTexts: JIRA ticket showing annual AWS Security Groups ruleset review was performed.
JIRA ticket showing Senior Director of Information Security and Compliance team provided sign off.

controls.title: Encryption - AWS
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has enabled customer managed keys (CMK) to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS EC2 instances. The keys are configured to be automatically rotated using AWS KMS every 365 days. Default encryption provided by AWS is enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS RDS instances and EBS volumes.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether customer managed keys (CMK) were enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS EC2 instances.

Additionally, inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether the customer managed keys (CMK) were configured to be automatically rotated every 365 days.

Further, inspected the configurations within AWS administrative console to determine whether default encryption provided by AWS was enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS RDS and EBS volumes.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing that customer managed keys (CMK) are enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS EC2 instances.
Screenshot showing that customer managed keys (CMK) are configured to be automatically rotated every 365 days
Screenshot showing that AWS is enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS RDS.
Screenshot showing that AWS is enabled to encrypt data-at-rest within AWS EBS Volumes.

controls.title: Encryption - Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio to transmit application data over the internet using Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) over TLS v1.2 / TLS v1.3.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products certificate to determine whether Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products were configured to transmit application data over the internet using Hypertext Transfer Protocol Secure (HTTPS) over TLS v1.2 or v1.3.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot showing that Zephyr Platform web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Quanta Self-Serve web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Quanta Analyze web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Quanta Discover web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Zephyr Console web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Quanta Assist web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3
Screenshot showing that Quanta Capture NG web application is configured to transmit data over HTTPS using TLSv1.2 or TLS v1.3

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - AWS Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: AWS CloudTrail is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within AWS Services using alerts. Alerts are configured by the CloudOps team to be re-directed to Datadog. Alerts that cross pre-defined thresholds have been configured to be sent as messages to Slack. The CloudOps team generates a JIRA ticket for confirmed true positive cases and tracks it to closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether alerts generated by AWS CloudTrail was configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether alerts that cross pre-defined thresholds were configured to be sent as messages to Slack.

Further, for a sample of JIRA tickets related to logging and monitoring of AWS Infrastructure, inspected the ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Datadog showing that AWS logs such as administrative events, authentication events, and system events etc. are being ingested by Datadog.
Screenshot from Datadog showing that alerts are sent to Meridian Slack based on pre-defined thresholds
System generated list of tickets from JIRA created for alerts regarding AWS Infrastructure logging and monitoring for  the audit period.
For a sample of alerts, Jira tickets that are raised.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio
controls.descriptionText: Luigi is utilized to identify administrative events, authentication events, and system events to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents within Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products using alerts. Alerts are configured by the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is configured to send a Slack message and raise a PagerDuty incident when thresholds are exceeded. The Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio Engineering team analyses these PagerDuty incidents and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within PagerDuty until closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Luigi to determine whether alerts generated by Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products were configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, inspected the configuration settings within Datadog to determine whether it was configured to automatically send a Slack message and raise PagerDuty incidents when thresholds were exceeded.

Further, for a sample of PagerDuty incidents related to logging and monitoring of Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products, inspected the ticket to determine whether the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio Engineering team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Luigi Task Status page showcasing that Luigi is used for monitoring for Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio logs
Screenshot from alert manager within Luigi showcasing that Datadog was configured to receive Luigi alerts
Screenshot showing that alerts are sent to Meridian Slack and PagerDuty based on pre-defined thresholds
System generated list of incidents from PagerDuty specific to logging and monitoring of Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products for the audit period.
For a sample of incidents, PagerDuty tickets showing that the incidents were analyzed and tracked to closure.

controls.title: Capacity management - AWS
controls.descriptionText: CAST AI is utilized to maintain Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio application availability and the capacity for critical production servers and databases hosted on AWS.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the CAST AI configurations defined for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio to determine whether CAST AI was utilized to maintain the capacity for critical production servers and databases hosted on AWS.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from CAST AI configuration files showing that CAST AI was utilized to maintain the capacity for critical production servers and databases hosted on AWS for all Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio accounts / subscriptions / instances.

controls.title: Reporting to User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has developed a status webpage (https://status.meridian.com) that offers visibility into the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio’s response and availability metrics across all cloud service providers and geographical instances. User entities can opt to subscribe to be notified of system incidents within this webpage.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the status webpage for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether application response and availability metrics across all cloud service providers and geographical instances were reported to the user entities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Zephyr Platform's web page showing that availability incidents, if present, are reported in real time within that web page.

controls.title: Support procedures for User Entities
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has prepared and maintained a support website (https://help.meridian.com/) containing resources such as product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums to help user entities effectively utilize Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the support website for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether resources such as product documentation, best practices, training materials, guides, troubleshooting tips, and community forums were documented.

controls.title: User Entity Onboarding
controls.descriptionText: During user entity onboarding, the Professional Services team informs the Engineering and CloudOps teams to provide access to the user entity through a Jira Ticket. Administrator privileges for the user entity account are provided only to personnel explicitly identified and authorized by the user entity. Once access is provisioned, ownership and management of the account are formally handed over to the user entity prior to closing the Jira ticket.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of user entities, inspected the JIRA ticket to determine whether a unique tenancy with administrator accounts to the personnel identified from the user entity organization was provisioned by the CloudOps team based on the details provided in the JIRA ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of customers onboarded during the audit period using all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products.

For a sample of customers, JIRA ticket/email communication showing a unique tenancy with administrator accounts to the personnel identified from the user entity organization was provisioned by the CloudOps team based on the details provided.
For a sample of customers, JIRA ticket/email communication showing that the customers were informed once they were onboarded.

controls.title: User Entity Offboarding
controls.descriptionText: Upon repatriation of a user entity, the Professional Services team informs the Customer Support team, based on which a Jira ticket tagging the Engineering and CloudOps team is raised. The AWS S3, AWS RDS, AWS Redshift, and MongoDB Databases of the repatriating user entity are deleted, and relevant details are documented within the Jira ticket. Data stored within the backup files are deleted in accordance with their respective backup retention configuration.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of repatriated user entities within the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products, inspected the Jira ticket to determine whether the CloudOps team deleted the AWS S3, AWS RDS, AWS Redshift, and MongoDB databases in accordance with the Meridian Tenant Deletion Policy.
evidenceRequirementTexts: List of customers offboarded during the audit period using all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products.
For a sample of offboarded customers, JIRA ticket showing that CloudOps team deleted the AWS S3, AWS RDS, AWS Redshift, and MongoDB databases.

controls.title: Customer Support Workflow
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has defined and documented a customer support workflow to identify and track the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the customer support workflow to determine whether the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to the  Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products were tracked.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Documentation showing that Meridian has customer support workflow documented to identify and track the reporting and resolution of problems or incidents related to Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products.

controls.title: Customer Support Tickets
controls.descriptionText: User entities can report issues by raising an issue from the front end of Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products, sending an email to “XYZ@meridian.example”, or raise a ticket via “https://help.XYZ.com”. These are recorded as customer support tickets within the DevRev Ticketing Tool. Agents within the Customer Support team analyses the customer query and provides resolution by coordinating with other teams within Meridian, as required. Upon resolution, the DevRev ticket is closed.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio incidents raised by user entities, inspected the DevRev ticket to determine whether resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of incidents from DevRev specific to logging and monitoring of Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products for the audit period.
For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets showing resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Data Ingestion, Processing, and Export
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured Luigi to monitor Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio platform for data ingestion, data duplication, data processing, and / or data export errors through alerts. Alerts are configured by the Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio Engineering team to be re-directed to Datadog, which is monitored by the Customer Support team. The Customer Support team analyses these Datadog alerts and raises DevRev tickets for true positive cases and documents the details of analysis, root cause, and prevention and mitigation steps within the DevRev ticket until closure.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the configuration settings within Luigi to determine whether alerts generated by Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio platform was configured to be re-directed to Datadog.

Additionally, for a sample of DevRev tickets related to data ingestion, data duplication, data processing, and / or data export errors, inspected the ticket to determine whether the Customer Support team analyzed and took necessary corrective actions prior to closing the ticket.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from Luigi Task Status page showcasing that Luigi is used for monitoring for Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio logs
Screenshot from alert manager within Luigi showcasing that Datadog was configured to receive Luigi alerts
System generated list of incidents from DevRev specific to logging and monitoring of Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products for the audit period.
For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets showing resolution was provided by the Customer Support team prior to closing the ticket.

controls.title: Data Ingestion Reports
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has configured a row level reconciliation process to execute before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data. Results of reconciliation are documented within a DevRev ticket.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of DevRev tickets related to data ingestion, inspected the ticket to determine whether a row level reconciliation process was executed before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of incidents from DevRev specific to logging and monitoring of Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products for the audit period.

For a sample of incidents, DevRev tickets related to data ingestion showing that a row level reconciliation process was executed before and after data ingestion to ensure the completeness and accuracy of the ingested data.

controls.title: New Hire Induction
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to sign the following documents at the time of joining to acknowledge the acceptance of terms to maintain the confidentiality and integrity of information within the organization:
1. Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC), and
2. Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA).
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the Appointment Letter and Non Disclosure Agreement (NDA) acknowledgement to determine whether the users had signed and agreed to preserve ethical values and the confidentiality and integrity of information within Meridian.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of new joiners from Workday for the audit period
For a sample of new joiners, Appointment Letter consisting of Code of Ethics and Business Conduct (COBC)
For a sample of new joiners, Non-Disclosure Agreement (NDA)

controls.title: Employee Handbook
controls.descriptionText: Formalized employee handbook is documented to communicate expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures. This document is published on U-Central and accessible by all users.
controls.testProcedure:
Performed inquiry with Senior HR Operations Specialist to determine whether formalized employee handbook was published on U-Central and accessible by all users.

Additionally, inspected the employee handbook to determine whether it communicated expected workforce conduct, organizational policies, and associated enforcement procedures.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Screenshot from U-Central showing that employee handbook was published on U-Central and is accessible by all users.
Employee Handbook

controls.title: Background and Reference Checks
controls.descriptionText:
The Human Resource team is responsible for carrying out background checks for new hires before the date of hire with the help of a third-party vendor, who conducts these checks and provides a background check report to Meridian. The following background checks are performed:
► International Criminal Searches,
► International Global Watchlist,
► International Education Verification, and
► International Employment Verification.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the background verification reports to determine whether background check was performed before the date of hire for parameters such as International Criminal Searches, International Global Watchlist, International Education Verification, and International Employment Verification.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners, BGV reports.

controls.title: Technical Interviews
controls.descriptionText: New hires are required to undergo technical interviews prior to appointment to help ensure that skill sets match with job requirements.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of new hires, inspected the technical interview assessment forms to determine whether the skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of new joiners for Bangalore & Chennai locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for London and Valencia locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

For a sample of new joiners for Palo Alto and New York locations, technical interview assessment forms showing that skill sets of the candidates were evaluated against the job requirements prior to the hiring of the candidate.

controls.title: User Trainings - New Hires
controls.descriptionText:
New hires are required to attend the following virtual trainings on Mindtickle platform during induction containing elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches:
1. Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct, and
2. Information Security and Compliance.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of new hires, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the new hires were made aware of code of conduct and information security and compliance during induction.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Global Code of Ethics and Business Conduct and Information Security and Compliance training material
For a sample of new joiners, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: User Trainings - Active Users
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, all active users are required to attend the following virtual Information Security and Compliance refresher trainings on Mindtickle platform, which includes information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the refresher Information Security and Compliance training material to determine whether it contained elements of information security policies, security threats overview, email security, password security, and incident management reporting mechanisms during breaches.

Additionally, for a sample of active users, inspected the training completion records within Mindtickle to determine whether the active users completed the refresher trainings.
evidenceRequirementTexts: System generated list of active users from Workday as on date
Refresher Information Security and Compliance training material for active users
For a sample of active users, the training completion records within Mindtickle

controls.title: Performance Evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Performance evaluations are carried out on a bi-annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.
controls.testProcedure: For a sample of active employees who have been in Meridian for more than six months, inspected the performance evaluation results within Workday to determine whether performance evaluations were carried out by employees and their reporting managers.
evidenceRequirementTexts: For a sample of active users, performance evaluation results within Workday

controls.title: VCSOM evaluation
controls.descriptionText: Vision, Culture, Strategy, Operating Model (VCSOM) evaluations are carried out on an annual basis for employees who have been in the organization for more than six months. The Human Resource team informs the employees and reporting managers to fill out VCSOM evaluation forms within Workday HRMS tool.

controls.title: Annual Risk Assessment
controls.descriptionText: The Information Security & Compliance team performs risk assessment of the various functions within Meridian with the support of internal Meridian stakeholders and prepares a risk register. Risks are identified based on inherent risk value, internal and external threats, vulnerabilities, likelihood, and potential impact to Meridian’s objectives, assets, and operations. Risks are evaluated based on impact magnitude and expectancy criteria in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability. Mitigating controls are identified to bring the inherent risk to acceptable levels and is documented within a Jira ticket.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the Risk Register and corresponding Jira ticket(s) to determine whether the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Latest Risk Register

Jira ticket(s) showing the Information Security & Compliance team identified, reviewed, and documented the risks associated with its various functions in context of CIS Critical Security Controls, information security, data integrity, confidentiality, and availability.

For a sample of vendors and third party service providers, vendor onboarding assessment reports

controls.title: Password and Account Lockout Policy – Active Directory
controls.descriptionText: Meridian has implemented Azure Active Directory to enforce password and account lockout policy settings on end user workstations. These policy settings include password length as 12 characters, password complexity, password history as 24 passwords remembered, and password expiry as 90 days. User accounts are locked after 5 invalid logon attempts and account lockout is reset after 30 minutes through group policy settings.

controls.title: Firewall Rules
controls.descriptionText:
Meridian has implemented perimeter firewalls on the Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia delivery center internet gateways to proactively identify and protect Meridian network against security, availability, confidentiality, and processing integrity threats from sources outside the system boundaries. Rules are enforced on the firewalls to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and
► configure Intrusion Prevention System (IPS) and web filters to monitor and prevent intrusion attempts.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the management approved network diagrams of Bengaluru, Chennai, London, Palo Alto, New York, and Valencia to determine whether firewall was implemented on the delivery center's internet gateway.

Additionally, inspected the configurations within the firewalls to determine whether rules were configured and enforced to:
► ‘deny all’ traffic by default with access enabled for specific services as per business requirements,
► protect against Distributed Denial of Service (DDoS) and IP spoofing attacks, and

controls.title: Source-Code Reviews
controls.descriptionText: Meridian conducts annual source code reviews through a third-party vendor to identify security vulnerabilities for the repositories related to the X Platform and the Quanta Portfolio products. The Information Security team raises JIRA tickets for high and critical severity issues, and the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
controls.testProcedure:
Inspected the annual source-code review report for Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether security vulnerabilities for the repositories were identified and documented.

Further, inspected the JIRA tickets for high and critical issues to determine whether the Engineering and Infrastructure Operations teams implement corrective actions within the defined SLAs. 

Additionally, inspected the email communication to determine whether A follow-up remediation review is performed by the vendor to ensure issues are resolved, reinforcing Meridian's commitment to secure coding practices and data protection.
evidenceRequirementTexts: JIRA tickets for high and critical issues identified in the source-code review reports.
Email communication/JIRA ticket/remediation report showing all issues are resolved.

controls.title: Vulnerability Assessment and Penetration Testing
controls.descriptionText: On an annual basis, third-party vendors conduct application level, network level, and API vulnerability assessments and penetration tests for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products. Based on the findings of these tests, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team follows up with the relevant stakeholders on appropriate corrective and preventive actions for identified gaps until closure of the gaps. Based on the confirmation received from the relevant stakeholders, Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team notifies the third-party vendor to perform rescan to confirm the closure of gaps through email. On completion of rescan, third-party vendor shares the summary report (Compliant Report) with the Information Security & Compliance / Security Operations team.
controls.testProcedure: Inspected the annual vulnerability assessment and penetration test reports for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio products to determine whether application level, network level, and API vulnerability and penetration tests for Zephyr Platform & Quanta Portfolio were conducted, and corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.
evidenceRequirementTexts: Annual Vulnerability assessment and penetration test (VAPT) reports for all Zephyr Platform and Quanta Portfolio products
Evidence showing that corrective and preventive actions were tracked to closure for identified vulnerabilities.

controls.title: Logging and Monitoring - IT Infrastructure
controls.descriptionText: Administrative events, authentication events, and system events generated by CrowdStrike, Microsoft MDM, and firewalls to identify inappropriate access, security threats, and incidents are configured by the Security Operations team to be re-directed to ProACT tool. The SISA MDR team analyses the alerts and generate a Freshdesk ticket for confirmed true positive cases. The Security Operations team analyses these Freshdesk tickets and documents the details of analysis, security violation, root cause, user / group responsible, severity of the incident / breach, and prevention and mitigation steps within the Freshdesk ticket until closure.

controls.title: Physical Access Reconciliation

The Workspace Experience team extracts the list of active users that have access to Meridian delivery centers from the access control software of that delivery center and compares this list with the list of active users shared by HR team.
